questions.question: WWI officially ended on November 11, 1918 at what time, now observed as a moment of silence?
questions.explanation: The Armistice ending WWI took effect at the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month (11 AM, November 11, 1918). This is why Remembrance Day / Veterans Day is observed at 11 AM on November 11. The war claimed approximately 20 million lives. Tragically, fighting continued right up until 11:00 AM — 11,000 casualties occurred in the final morning alone.
questions.memory_hook: 11th hour, 11th day, 11th month. The most symmetrical ceasefire in history. Men were dying at 10:59 AM and alive at 11:01. The last soldier killed in WWI died at 10:59:59 — one minute before peace.

questions.question: The Trojan Horse was a military stratagem used by the Greeks against which city?
questions.explanation: According to Greek mythology (and possibly based on historical events around 1200 BC), the Greeks ended their 10-year siege of Troy by hiding soldiers inside a giant wooden horse, which the Trojans brought inside their city walls as a trophy. That night, the Greek soldiers emerged and opened the city gates. The story is told in Homer's Iliad and Odyssey.
questions.memory_hook: Trojan Horse = trick gift. The Greeks couldn't break Troy's walls in 10 years, so they built a horse instead. The Trojans dragged their own doom inside the gates. 'Beware of Greeks bearing gifts' — the oldest lesson in not trusting suspicious presents.

questions.question: The Prohibition era in the United States, banning alcohol, lasted from 1920 to which year?
questions.explanation: Prohibition — the nationwide ban on alcohol production and sale — was enacted by the 18th Amendment in 1920 and repealed by the 21st Amendment in 1933. Rather than eliminating drinking, it created a booming underground economy of speakeasies, bootleggers, and organized crime. Al Capone's criminal empire was largely built on illegal alcohol sales during this period.
questions.memory_hook: Prohibition: 1920–1933, thirteen years. The US banned alcohol and accidentally created the Mafia as we know it. Al Capone made more money from illegal booze than most legitimate companies. The cure created a worse disease.

questions.question: The Cold War — a political and military standoff between the US and Soviet Union — lasted roughly from 1947 to which year?
questions.explanation: The Cold War is generally considered to have ended in 1991 with the dissolution of the Soviet Union on December 25, 1991. The conflict was characterized by nuclear arms races, proxy wars, and ideological competition between capitalism and communism. The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, which marked the beginning of the end, but the USSR formally collapsed two years later.
questions.memory_hook: Cold War ended 1991 = Soviet Union dissolved. The USSR collapsed on Christmas Day 1991. The world's largest country — spanning 11 time zones — ceased to exist on December 25. The most dramatic Christmas gift in geopolitical history.

questions.question: Which country has won the most Summer Olympic gold medals in the history of the Games?
questions.explanation: The United States has won more Summer Olympic gold medals than any other country, with over 1,000 gold medals across all Summer Games. The US has topped the overall medal count at the majority of Olympics it has attended. Track and field, swimming, and gymnastics are traditionally among the US's strongest events.
questions.memory_hook: USA = most Olympic gold overall. The US boycotted 1980 Moscow, the USSR boycotted 1984 LA in retaliation. Even with two missed Games, the US still leads all-time. Michael Phelps alone won more golds than most countries have ever won.

questions.question: The first Super Bowl was played in 1967 between the Green Bay Packers and which team?
questions.explanation: Super Bowl I was played on January 15, 1967, at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum. The Green Bay Packers defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 35-10. The game was broadcast on both NBC and CBS simultaneously — the only Super Bowl to air on two networks. Quarterback Bart Starr was named MVP. Green Bay coach Vince Lombardi is the reason the Super Bowl trophy is called the Lombardi Trophy.
questions.memory_hook: First Super Bowl: Packers beat Chiefs. The Chiefs lost Super Bowl I in 1967 and had to wait over 50 years before winning one in 2020. They've since become a dynasty — bookending their history with Super Bowl appearances.

questions.question: Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal, and Novak Djokovic have dominated men's tennis for decades. Which player holds the most Grand Slam singles titles?
questions.explanation: Novak Djokovic holds the record for the most Grand Slam singles titles in men's tennis history with 24, surpassing Rafael Nadal (22) and Roger Federer (20). Djokovic won his 24th Slam at the 2023 US Open. The three players — often called the 'Big Three' — have collectively won the vast majority of Grand Slam titles since 2003.
questions.memory_hook: Djokovic 24, Nadal 22, Federer 20. The Big Three traded the record between themselves for 20 years. Federer held it first, Nadal tied and passed him, Djokovic passed them both. The greatest rivalry in sports history — and it went three rounds.

questions.question: Citizen Kane (1941), often called the greatest film ever made, was directed by and starred which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Orson Welles directed, co-wrote, and starred as Charles Foster Kane in Citizen Kane (1941), which he made at age 25 for RKO Pictures. The film pioneered numerous cinematic techniques including deep focus photography and non-linear storytelling. Despite being largely overlooked at the time of release, it has topped most 'Greatest Films Ever Made' lists for decades.
questions.memory_hook: Citizen Kane = Orson Welles at 25. He made the 'greatest film ever made' at an age when most directors haven't even made their first film. It flopped on release. The studio hated it. History had the last word.

questions.question: Which legendary singer's real name was Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta?
questions.explanation: Lady Gaga was born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta in New York City in 1986. Her stage name was inspired by the Queen song 'Radio Ga Ga.' Known for her theatrical performances and avant-garde fashion, she has sold over 170 million records worldwide and won multiple Grammy Awards. She also demonstrated dramatic acting range in A Star Is Born (2018), earning an Oscar nomination.
questions.memory_hook: Gaga = Germanotta. The name 'Lady Gaga' came from Queen's 'Radio Ga Ga.' She took a rock radio song and made it her entire identity. Stefani Germanotta from New York became Lady Gaga the art performance — one of music's great transformations.

questions.question: Which film franchise holds the record as the highest-grossing of all time?
questions.explanation: The Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) is the highest-grossing film franchise in history, with over $29 billion in worldwide box office revenue across more than 30 films as of 2023. The franchise began with Iron Man (2008) and has expanded to include The Avengers, Black Panther, Spider-Man, and many others. Avengers: Endgame (2019) is the second highest-grossing film of all time at $2.8 billion.
questions.memory_hook: MCU = biggest franchise in history. Marvel turned interconnected superhero movies into a genre-defining machine. Before Iron Man in 2008, no one thought a shared movie universe could work. Now every studio has tried to copy it.
